text.title: IaC Pull-Request Review Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the iac-pr-review-checklist methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: PR description names the blast radius (modules + environments affected) and the rollback path before review starts.
rule.rationale: Without a named blast radius, the reviewer cannot reason about acceptable change risk.

rule.statement: PR carries the terraform plan / helm diff / kustomize build output as a comment or artefact, dated within the review window.
rule.rationale: Reviewing source without plan is reviewing intent without effect — the divergence is where incidents live.

rule.statement: PRs that touch state movement (terraform state mv / rm) or destructive replacements MUST flag the mutation in the title and require two approvers.
rule.rationale: State mutations are not retriable; the title-flag is the documented attention-grabbing convention.

rule.statement: The diff MUST not contain plaintext secrets, key material, or cloud account IDs outside the documented allow-list.
rule.rationale: Once secrets land in a public PR they are burned even if reverted — never-in-diff is the only safe rule.

rule.statement: Any IAM change is explicit (no resource = "*"), uses managed policies where possible, and the PR enumerates new permissions in plain language.
rule.rationale: IAM is the recurring breach root cause; explicit + plain-language change forces the reviewer to actually read it.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
